headline: SpaceX lines up April investor briefings as IPO questions swirl
summary: (Bloomberg) -- SpaceX is telling prospective IPO investors to expect briefings in April from company executives, according to people familiar with the matter, as advisers scramble to file for potentially the biggest listing of all time.Billionaire Elon Musk’s space and artificial intelligence company is set to hold so-called testing-the-waters investor meetings in the weeks after the Easter holiday, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. headline: Exelon Could Reach $58 by End of 2026 as AI Data Center Demand Fuels 26% Load Growth in Illinois
summary: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) has gained 9.90% year-to-date and 9.65% over the past year, with shares currently trading at $48.27. The stock hit a 52-week high of $50.65 earlier in March, still well below where one major Wall Street firm now sees it heading. The Street’s average price target sits at $50.88, with 11 of 20 ... headline: Why The BOK Financial (BOKF) Story Is Shifting With New Targets And Valuation Caution
summary: Price targets for BOK Financial have been reset higher by several bullish analysts, with moves ranging from mid to high single and double digit dollar increases. These shifts sit alongside a valuation driven downgrade to Neutral from more cautious research, creating a mixed backdrop where optimism on execution meets concern that the current share price may already be close to perceived fair value. As you read on, you will see how these evolving targets and opinions may help you track the next...

headline: Federal Reserve urges judge to deny bid to revive Jerome Powell probe subpoenas
summary: President Donald Trump, in his latest jibe at Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, called him a "moron,' and blasted him over keeping interest rates too high.

raw.headline: Federal Reserve urges judge to deny bid to revive Jerome Powell probe subpoenas

headline: Google Breakthrough Spurs Chip Selloff Despite Analyst Doubt
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Shares of computer memory and storage products slumped on concerns over demand after Google researchers touted a new compression technique. But it may be a hiccup rather than an existential threat.SK Hynix Inc., a key maker of memory chips for artificial intelligence applications, fell as much as 6.4% on the Korea Exchange. Flash memory manufacturer Kioxia Holdings Corp. dropped 6.4% in Tokyo. That followed losses by Micron Technology Inc. and Sandisk Corp. Wednesday in New York.T

headline: Ardagh Metal Packaging, Apple And Netflix On CNBC's 'Final Trades'
summary: Jenny Harrington recommends Ardagh Metal Packaging for its 9.5% dividend yield. Citigroup, Netflix and Apple also receive positive ratings.
